items.headline: State grant funds Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: The City Council approved funding from the California Natural Resources Agency for the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This supports cultural programming in San José.

items.headline: City updates Active Transportation Plan with revised scoring criteria
items.summary: The City Council received an update on Phase 2 of the Active Transportation Plan, including edits and new scoring criteria that will guide future biking and walking infrastructure projects. This plan shapes which streets get bike lanes, sidewalk improvements, and pedestrian safety upgrades.

items.headline: Council considers changes to vacancy ordinance rules
items.summary: The City Council is introducing an ordinance to amend how council vacancies are handled, though specific details of the changes are not fully outlined in the agenda. Residents interested in how council seats are filled should monitor this item.

items.headline: Major elevator upgrade project approved downtown
items.summary: A $1.1 million elevator upgrade project at 1290 Ridder Park Dr signals significant infrastructure investment in San José's commercial district. This type of major modernization typically indicates planned expansion or repositioning of an office or mixed-use building.

items.headline: Garden Gate Coalition lawsuit against city over Mary Avenue continues
items.summary: The city is in ongoing litigation with the Garden Gate Coalition regarding Mary Avenue safety concerns. This active court case (26CV488611) involves disputes over street design or safety measures on that major corridor.

items.headline: Commercial/industrial development underway on West San Carlos
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial or industrial project at 1300 W San Carlos St represents new business activity in San José's West Side corridor. The project could bring new employment or services to this developing area.

items.headline: Gas utility rates amended; reserve transfer approved
items.summary: The City Council approved changes to residential and commercial gas service rates and authorized a reserve transfer for the gas utility's fiscal year 2027 budget. Residents should expect to see these rate changes reflected in upcoming utility bills.

items.headline: City launches business amnesty program and retail incentives
items.summary: Cupertino is rolling out a Business License Amnesty Program for shopping centers, new outreach initiatives, and economic development incentives targeting retail stores, small businesses, and startups. This suggests the city is trying to formalize unlicensed businesses and attract commercial activity.
